title: Plushes in EoE Theater Scene
wikitext:
[]: Plushes in EoE Theater Scene             Quote Post by Cirk » Wed Dec 24, 2014 12:28 pm I don't know how many times that has been brought up on here, but yeah, I noticed a guy with Rei and Asuka plushes during the theater scene in EoE. I wonder if they were custom made or in UFO Catchers in Japan or something, but I've never seen them before.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Wed Dec 24, 2014 3:13 pm And I thought EoE couldn't have possibly gotten more meta.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 24, 2014 6:22 pm I've always wondered what those people were watching.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 24, 2014 6:46 pm I want them. They’re so adorable. Chuckman wrote: I've always wondered what those people were watching.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 24, 2014 8:04 pm I once read a source (sadly long forgotten what) that they're watching Death and Rebirth , yeah. Those look like your run of the mill UFO catchers, as far as I can tell.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Wed Dec 24, 2014 9:09 pm Yeah I remember seeing the plushes, but what I remember most about that scene was the guy doing what he thought were gang signals or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 24, 2014 9:30 pm Another thing: That is a fuckhuge theater. Has anyone seen a movie in Japan? I'm curious to know what the behavior and decorum in a theater is like.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Dec 24, 2014 9:55 pm From what little I heard on the subject the audiences in Japan are reserved

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Dec 25, 2014 10:48 am Chuckman wrote: Has anyone seen a movie in Japan? I'm curious to know what the behavior and decorum in a theater is like.

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Thu Dec 25, 2014 11:36 am I always thought talking during the movies in a cinema was a dumb American thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Dec 25, 2014 11:56 am vito wrote: I always thought talking during the movies in a cinema was a dumb American thing?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Dec 25, 2014 1:05 pm vito wrote: I always thought talking during the movies in a cinema was a dumb American thing?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Dec 25, 2014 2:03 pm Sometimes a collective reaction out of the audience is nice. Joker's magic trick in TDK was fantastic in a full theater on opening day.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 25, 2014 5:17 pm robersora wrote: Though, right after the credits started there was a loud scream of "what the hell was that?!" and another "ANOOOooo~~~". People laughed, but then left the theater without much talking.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Dec 25, 2014 9:44 pm I thought silence was the ultimate sign of respect for a film. I remember in a documentary about Star Wars called Empire of Dreams that when A New Hope premiered in Japan, the Fox executives that were present for the screening thought the audience didn't like it because they were quiet. They were told that silence indicates respect. Did they get that completely wrong?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Dec 25, 2014 11:10 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: It was said in Japanese, or...? Just curious. :) Ornette mentioned when he saw 1.0 and 2.0, people applauded during the credits. But for 3.0 credits, it was dead silence. One Japanese fan described the atmosphere after 3.0 was like "being at a funeral", it felt that serious/somber.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Dec 25, 2014 11:16 pm I usually only go to an actual theater to see big blockbusters so I sort of expect and welcome a bit of audience reaction. I was actually disappointed during the showing of Godzilla I attended (last time I've been in a theater) that there wasn't any audience reaction to the big roar scene or the atomic breath. Most fun I've ever had in a theater was probably Independence Day and the Matrix sequels. Lot of laughing and cheering. Sounds like a Japanese theater would be very dull.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Dec 26, 2014 2:00 am Chuckman wrote: Most fun I've ever had in a theater was probably Independence Day

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sat Dec 27, 2014 4:21 am I saw Q in Shibuya a couple days after release and went to the same complex last month for Interstellar . Nothing to much to add to what Rob said; audiences are super quiet, respectful and sit through the credits (which I think is really cool). I agree with the anecdote of the atmosphere being like a funeral after Q , there wasn't a dry eye in the house either. It was cool to kind of imagine then what it would've been like to have seen EOE in cinemas as well, after effects and all. Chuckman wrote: I usually only go to an actual theater to see big blockbusters so I sort of expect and welcome a bit of audience reaction. I was actually disappointed during the showing of Godzilla I attended (last time I've been in a theater) that there wasn't any audience reaction to the big roar scene or the atomic breath.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Tue Dec 30, 2014 3:49 am Chuckman wrote: I've always wondered what those people were watching.
summary:
[]: Plushes in EoE Theater Scene             Quote Post by Cirk » Wed Dec 24, 2014 12:28 pm I don't know how many times that has been brought up on here, but yeah, I noticed a guy with Rei and Asuka plushes during the theater scene in EoE. I wonder if they were custom made or in UFO Catchers in Japan or something, but I've never seen them before.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Na
